content.thinking: Open `scraper.js`.
The code uses `playwright`. It imports `chromium`. But the project has a `playwright` dependency. Let's inspect `package.json`.

They didn't add a normal dependency but devDependency. Node will still install it. But the script might fail due to missing `playwright` path or missing browser binaries. Usually `playwright` installs its own browsers. But maybe the script fails due to something else.

Let's run `node scraper.js`. We can't run code directly but we can simulate. Use bash to run node.

Also maybe the script should use `row.$('a.storylink')` not `td.title > a.storylink`. But that's fine.

content.thinking: All rows have no titleHandle? That's weird. Maybe the selector is wrong: `td.title > a.titleline`. The anchor is inside `td.title` but not direct child? Actually the structure: `Title...`. So `td.title > a.titleline` fails because `a` is nested inside `span`. Should use `td.title span.titleline > a`. Let's use `row.$('td.title span.titleline > a')`. Let's patch.

edits.newText: const titleHandle = await row.$('td.title span.titleline > a');
